title: rosa canina subvar. calophylla
intro: Rosa canina subvar. calophylla, commonly known as the Scotch rose or the wild rose of Shetland, is a subvariety of the Rosa canina species, a deciduous shrub native to the Northern Hemisphere. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The therapeutic actions of Rosa canina subvar. calophylla are attributed to its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of the plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
benefits: Rosa canina subvar. calophylla has many benefits, such as providing high-quality antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions, including arthritis, diabetes, and cardiovascular disease. The plant's extracts have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it effective in wound healing, skin care, and oral health, reducing the risk of infections and promoting overall well-being. Additionally, the plant's leaves and flowers are rich in vitamins, minerals, and essential fatty acids, supporting eye health, boosting the immune system, and potentially lowering cholesterol levels. By harnessing the benefits of Rosa canina subvar. calophylla, individuals can experience improved physical health, enhanced cognitive function, and a better quality of life.
actions: Rosa canina subvar. calophylla has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, reduce oxidative stress, and protect against cell damage. Its antiseptic and antimicrobial properties can aid in wound healing, prevent infections, and promote healthy skin. Additionally, its antispasmodic and anxiolytic properties can help manage anxiety disorders, reduce muscle spasms, and improve sleep quality, ultimately leading to enhanced overall well-being and improved daily functioning. By harnessing the therapeutic actions of Rosa canina subvar. calophylla, individuals can experience relief from chronic pain, improved mental health, and a better quality of life.
intro_study: According to "African journal of traditional, complementary, and alternative medicines : AJTCAM", rosa canina subvar. calophylla has shown to have significant antioxidant and α-amylase inhibition activities, which could be beneficial in the management of diabetes mellitus.
constituents: Rosa canina subvar. calophylla has many active constituents, such as flavonoids, including quercetin, kaempferol, and isorhapontigenin, which possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. The plant also contains phenolic acids, like gallic acid and ellagic acid, which exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activities, aiding in the prevention of infections and promoting wound healing. Additionally, Rosa canina subvar. calophylla is rich in essential oils, including geraniol and linalool, which have been shown to reduce stress and anxiety levels, improving overall mental well-being and promoting a good night's sleep. The various medicinal constituents present in Rosa canina subvar. calophylla work together to provide a range of health benefits, contributing to improved cardiovascular health, enhanced cognitive function, and a stronger immune system.
parts: Rosa canina subvar. calophylla has many medicinal parts, such as the flowers, hips, and leaves. The flowers of Rosa canina subvar. calophylla contain flavonoids like quercetin, which has anti-inflammatory properties, and anthocyanins, which are known for their antioxidant effects. The hips, or fruit, of the plant are rich in vitamin C and contain carotenoids like beta-carotene, which is converted to vitamin A in the body and is essential for healthy vision and immune function. The leaves of Rosa canina subvar. calophylla contain tannins, which have astringent properties, and phenolic acids like salicylic acid, which is known for its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects.
preparations: Rosa canina subvar. calophylla has many herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, that are used for medicinal purposes. A tea made from the rose hips of Rosa canina subvar. calophylla is high in vitamin C and is used to boost the immune system and treat scurvy, while a tincture is used to treat digestive issues and inflammation. Infusions made from the flowers of the plant are used to treat anxiety and insomnia, while the essential oil extracted from the plant is used to treat skin conditions such as acne and eczema. Additionally, a syrup made from the rose hips is used to treat respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis.
side_effects: Rosa canina subvar. calophylla can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing allergic reactions, skin irritation, and digestive issues in some individuals. Ingesting large amounts of the plant's extract can lead to stomach cramps, diarrhea, and vomiting. Additionally, applying the plant's oil topically can cause skin redness and inflammation, especially in those with sensitive skin. Improper use of Rosa canina subvar. calophylla can also interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications, leading to adverse health effects.
